excerpt: The proof of the divinity of the Creator is found in the distinction between divinity and creation; creation refers to the transformation of existing substances (like wood into a chair), while divinity pertains to bringing forth from absolute non-existence.
source_location_hint: Opening discussion of divinity and creation

excerpt: In his book Raḥat al-ʿAql (The Repose of the Intellect), Ḥamīd al-Dīn al-Kirmānī negates all the reasons for creation and proves that the Creator brought the whole world only as a means of grace, forgiveness and reward; it is in the nature of the Creator to bestow grace without reason or purpose.
source_location_hint: Discussion of the reason for divinity and creation

excerpt: The real purpose of creation is worship (ʿibāda), and the essence of all worship is bearing witness to the unity of the Lord of all beings (tawhīd Rabb al-ʿālamīn). For true worship requires that the servant know himself and thereby recognize his Lord.
source_location_hint: Discussion of the purpose of creation

excerpt: The Creator brought forth the spiritual world from non-existence in a single instantaneous act, populating it with luminous beings that were brought into existence from the source of divine clemency and grace.
source_location_hint: On the creation of the spiritual world

excerpt: The Creator fashioned all beings in the form of Alif, because Alif is the most perfect and primary of all letters in the alphabet. Later, when He created humanity in the physical world, He preserved this same primordial form.
source_location_hint: On the form of Alif in creation

excerpt: The spiritual world is known by seven names reflecting different aspects of creation: World of Existence, World of Command, World of Knowledge, The Spiritual World, World of Origination, World of Subtlety, and World of Purity.

Primary perfection consists of three jewels: eternal life, absolute power, and comprehensive knowledge. All beings received these three essences in potential form, and knowledge was essential so that these beings could exercise their capacities.
source_location_hint: On the three jewels of primary perfection
